zh.Woodford, Northamptonshire, England: 英國諾薩普頓史爾伍德福特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Hynton於1190年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾伍德福特時，他的父親Hugh de Hynton of Hinton-Woodford30歲，母親Matilda Maud Foliot30歲。他和Arelina至少有1個兒子。他於1249年在英國英格蘭賀福爾被列為knighted by king henry iii of england。他去世於1253年享年63歲。

zh-hans.Woodford, Northamptonshire, England: 英国諾薩普頓史爾伍德福特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Hynton于1190年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾伍德福特时，他的父亲Hugh de Hynton of Hinton-Woodford30岁，母亲Matilda Maud Foliot30岁。他和Arelina至少有1个儿子。他于1249年在英国英格兰賀福爾被列为knighted by king henry iii of england。他去世于1253年享年63岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hynton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the many places called Hinton (for example, in Shropshire, Herefordshire, Gloucestershire, Somerset, Hampshire, and Northamptonshire). Some of the placenames, such as those in Northamptonshire, Shropshire, and Herefordshire, derive from Old English hīna, genitive plural form of hīwan ‘household, religious community’, + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’ (compare Hine as the first element). Others, such as those in Gloucestershire, Somerset, and Hampshire, derive from Old English hēan, dative form of hēah ‘high’ + tūn.

variants.name: Hine
meanings: English (Devon): occupational name from Middle English, Old English hīne ‘servant, member of a household’, also ‘farm laborer’ (such as a herdsman or shepherd). Originally a collective term for a body of servants, from an Old English plural noun, hīwan ‘household’, in early modern English it commonly acquired excrescent -d (see Hinde ). Evidence from Nottinghamshire shows that some bearers of the name were wealthy freemen, which suggests that their eponymous ancestors were not farmhands but senior members of a lord's household. From the late 15th century onward in western England, from south to north, a hine or hind also denoted a farm steward or manager, a sense that may be relevant to the surname in northwestern England, where hereditary naming had not been fully established by the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Hynton was born in 1190, in Woodford, Northamptonshire, England, his father, Hugh de Hynton of Hinton-Woodford, was 30 and his mother, Matilda Maud Foliot, was 30. He had at least 1 son with Arelina. In 1249, his occupation is listed as knighted by king henry iii of england in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom. He died in 1253, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Hynton est né en 1190, à Woodford, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Hugh de Hynton of Hinton-Woodford, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Matilda Maud Foliot, avait 30 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Arelina. En 1249, sa profession de son indiquée est knighted by king henry iii of england à Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé en 1253, à l’âge de 63 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Hynton 1190 in Woodford, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hugh de Hynton of Hinton-Woodford 30 und seine Mutter Matilda Maud Foliot 30. Er hatte mit Arelina mindestens 1 Sohn. 1249 ist sein Beruf als knighted by king henry iii of england in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb 1253 im Alter von 63 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Hynton è nato il nel 1190 nel Woodford, Northamptonshire, England, il suo padre, Hugh de Hynton of Hinton-Woodford, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Matilda Maud Foliot, aveva 30 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Arelina. Nel 1249, la sua occupazione è indicata come knighted by king henry iii of england nel Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì nel 1253, all'età di 63 anni.

ja.Woodford, Northamptonshire, England: イギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアウッドフォード
ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Hyntonが1190年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアウッドフォードで生まれた時，父Hugh de Hynton of Hinton-Woodfordは30歳，母Matilda Maud Foliotは30歳であった。彼はArelinaとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。1249年，イギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアでの職業はknighted by king henry iii of englandと記録されている。彼は1253年に63歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Hynton(이)가 1190년 영국 노르섬프토실르 우드퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh de Hynton of Hinton-Woodford(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda Maud Foliot(은)는 30세였다. 그는 Arelina(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 1249년, 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르에서 knighted by king henry iii of england이었다. 그는 1253년 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Hynton nasceu em 1190, em Woodford, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Hugh de Hynton of Hinton-Woodford, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Matilda Maud Foliot, tinha 30 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Arelina. Em 1249, sua profissão aparece como knighted by king henry iii of england em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 1253, com 63 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Hynton родился в 1190 г. в местности вудфорд, нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Hugh de Hynton of Hinton-Woodford было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda Maud Foliot, 30. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Arelina. В 1249 году он работал по профессии knighted by king henry iii of england в местности хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер в 1253 г. в возрасте 63 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Hynton nació en 1190, en Woodford, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Hugh de Hynton of Hinton-Woodford, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Matilda Maud Foliot, tenía 30 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Arelina. En 1249, su ocupación registrada era knighted by king henry iii of england en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió en 1253, a la edad de 63 años.
